evals.prompt: Write a one-page partnership memo to Rainmatter Foundation about Carbon Bridge™. We're looking to deepen the partnership for the next field season. Keep it on-brand.
evals.expected_output: An HTML or markdown memo in C-GEM voice: no hype words, no 'beneficiaries', programme name with ™, Indian numerals, Newsreader italic voice moments, correct brand strings, funder-appropriate tone.

assertions.name: no-beneficiaries
assertions.description: The word 'beneficiar' (covers beneficiary/beneficiaries) does not appear anywhere in the output

assertions.name: programme-trademark
assertions.description: Carbon Bridge™ appears with the trademark symbol

assertions.name: brand-string-exact
assertions.description: C-GEM appears hyphenated and in caps (not CGEM or c-gem)

assertions.name: no-hype-words
assertions.description: None of: revolutionary, game-changing, disruptive, transformative, powerful, unlock appear in the output

assertions.name: contains-data-point
assertions.description: At least one concrete number or verifiable fact appears (e.g. 2,71,000 or 14 states or 5 projects)
assertions.value: Does the output contain at least one concrete data point such as a number of farming families, states, or projects? Answer yes or no.

evals.prompt: Create an HTML stat section I can drop into the C-GEM homepage. Should show our three headline numbers with the right design system styling.
evals.expected_output: Self-contained HTML snippet using C-GEM design tokens: --leaf-600, --sprout, Geist Mono, tabular-nums, Indian-format numerals (2,71,000 / 5 / 14), .stat and .unit classes, .eyebrow label, white ground.

assertions.name: indian-numeral-format
assertions.description: 2,71,000 appears (not 271,000 or 2.71 lakh or 271000)

assertions.name: design-token-leaf
assertions.description: References --leaf-600 or #2C7A3D (C-GEM primary brand green)

assertions.name: tabular-nums
assertions.description: tabular-nums appears in the CSS for numeric figures

assertions.name: stat-class-used
assertions.description: .stat class or stat styling applied to numeric figures

assertions.name: white-ground
assertions.description: Background uses white (#FFFFFF or --paper), not grey or cream

evals.prompt: Draft a LinkedIn post announcing that C-GEM now works across 14 Indian states. Should feel authentic, not corporate.
evals.expected_output: Short LinkedIn post: no emoji, no hype words, Indian numeral grouping where used, C-GEM hyphenated and caps, farmers as protagonists not 'beneficiaries', authentic declarative voice.

assertions.name: no-emoji
assertions.description: No emoji characters appear in the post
assertions.value: Does the output contain any emoji characters? Answer yes (contains emoji) or no (no emoji).

assertions.name: no-beneficiaries
assertions.description: The word 'beneficiar' does not appear

assertions.name: no-hype-words
assertions.description: None of: revolutionary, game-changing, disruptive, transformative appear

assertions.name: brand-string-exact
assertions.description: C-GEM appears hyphenated and caps

assertions.name: farmers-as-protagonists
assertions.description: Farmers described with active agency — not as passive recipients
assertions.value: Are farming communities / smallholder farmers described with active agency (e.g. they lead, negotiate, own, protect, thrive) rather than as passive recipients (e.g. they receive, access, benefit from, are helped)? Answer yes or no.

evals.prompt: Build an HTML programme overview page for Carbon Ready™ targeting FPOs and grassroots NGOs exploring carbon markets for the first time.
evals.expected_output: Full HTML page using marketing skin: Geist fonts, --harvest accent for Carbon Ready™, .voice Newsreader italic moments, .eyebrow labels, .lede intro, .stat blocks, audience-appropriate warm plain language, white ground, no hype.

assertions.name: harvest-accent
assertions.description: Uses --harvest or #C98A1F for Carbon Ready™ accent colour

assertions.name: programme-trademark
assertions.description: Carbon Ready™ appears with trademark symbol

assertions.name: no-beneficiaries
assertions.description: The word 'beneficiar' does not appear

assertions.name: geist-font-referenced
assertions.description: Geist font is loaded or referenced (marketing skin requirement)

assertions.name: voice-class-or-newsreader
assertions.description: .voice class or Newsreader font used for a quote or lede moment

assertions.name: no-hype-words
assertions.description: None of: revolutionary, game-changing, disruptive, transformative, synergies, leverage appear
